funFacts: Moray eels have a second set of jaws located in their throats (pharyngeal jaws), which shoot forward to drag prey into their stomachs.
Designed for Easy difficulty, this specific sheet is perfect for practicing clean borders and line work using high-resolution colored pencils.
Coloring this drawing is a fantastic way to learn about advanced mechanical structure and shapes while developing fine motor coordination.
